perturbation.original_text: 1.4 Contract Term The commencement date for this contract will be 16th June 2004. The initial term of the contracted agreement is 5 years from the commencement date. The BSP then have the option to renew the agreement for another 5 years subject to any restructuring of the agreement as required by the parties. Either party may advise the other of the impending expiry of the then current term, generally in accordance with section 14 .
perturbation.changed_text: 1.4 Contract Term The commencement date for this contract will be 16th June 2004. The initial term of the contracted agreement is 5 years from the commencement date. The BSP then have the option to renew the agreement for another 2 years subject to any restructuring of the agreement as required by the parties. Either party may advise the other of the impending expiry of the then current term, generally in accordance with section 14 .
perturbation.explanation: The change in Section 1.4 modifies the contract renewal term from '5 years' to '2 years'. This in-text contradiction creates uncertainty regarding the actual duration of the renewal period, which can lead to disputes over the length of the contract extension. This directly contradicts section 14, which refers back to the terms outlined for termination based on the originally stated contract term lengths.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 14.3 Termination By BSP For Convenience The BSP may terminate the whole or any part of this agreement for convenience at any time by giving Datec at least 6 months prior written notice.
perturbation.justification: The modified text in Section 1.4 changes the renewal term from 5 years to 2 years. This directly contradicts the implied timeline within Section 14.3, which discusses termination with a 6-month notice, implicitly referencing the initial 5-year term and any subsequent renewals of *that* duration. By shortening the renewal, the basis for calculating termination notice based on the originally defined term length is altered, potentially creating uncertainty or disputes about the applicable notice period, constituting a strong in-text contradiction as per the provided definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: 2.10 Technology (Equipment) Refresh Planning And Implementation Datec will be responsible for liaison with managed third parties (MTP's) in the procurement and management of installations, and deletion of and Tracking Network Services within the Network under instruction from BSP. Such responsibilities will include: (a) Management of the installation of Network Services; (b) Procurement of upgrades, changes or deletion of Network Services, as appropriate to accommodate BSP's changing requirements; (c) interfacing with the appropriate Network Services providers for problems related to Network Services; and (d) tracking relevant Network Service information . Datec will be responsible for supporting, administering, managing and performing provisioning of Equipment and Equipment Software comprising the Network. Unless specifically exempted, the equipment is to be supplied by Datec and invoiced for payment according to Datec's normal terms of trade. Datec will: (a) install Network Equipment and Equipment Software and monitor the fulfilment of each order for accuracy; (b) store, deliver and unpack all items of Equipment and remove and dispose of all packaging; (c) configure, install and test all Equipment and Equipment Software and transfer data and Equipment Software configurations as required prior to commissioning in the production environment (d) provide connectivity for the Equipment and Equipment Software at, or prior to, the time of installation; (e) remove BSP Equipment Software from displaced or retired Equipment to the extent required by BSP's security procedures; (f) remove displaced or retired Equipment and Equipment Software; and (g) ensure BSP is aware of all needs to promptly disconnect and end Network Services and remove related Equipment no longer needed by BSP upon termination of Services at a Site. Datec agrees to remove the applicable Equipment.
perturbation.changed_text: 2.10 Technology (Equipment) Refresh Planning And Implementation Datec will be responsible for liaison with managed third parties (MTP's) in the procurement and management of installations, and deletion of and Tracking Network Services within the Network under instruction from BSP. Such responsibilities will include: (a) Management of the installation of Network Services; (b) Procurement of upgrades, changes or deletion of Network Services, as appropriate to accommodate BSP's changing requirements; (c) interfacing with the appropriate Network Services providers for problems related to Network Services; and (d) tracking relevant Network Service information . Datec will be responsible for supporting, administering, managing and performing provisioning of Equipment and Equipment Software comprising the Network. Unless specifically exempted, the equipment is to be supplied by BSP and not invoiced for payment. Datec will: (a) install Network Equipment and Equipment Software and monitor the fulfilment of each order for accuracy; (b) store, deliver and unpack all items of Equipment and remove and dispose of all packaging; (c) configure, install and test all Equipment and Equipment Software and transfer data and Equipment Software configurations as required prior to commissioning in the production environment (d) provide connectivity for the Equipment and Equipment Software at, or prior to, the time of installation; (e) remove BSP Equipment Software from displaced or retired Equipment to the extent required by BSP's security procedures; (f) remove displaced or retired Equipment and Equipment Software; and (g) ensure BSP is aware of all needs to promptly disconnect and end Network Services and remove related Equipment no longer needed by BSP upon termination of Services at a Site. Datec agrees to remove the applicable Equipment.
perturbation.explanation: This in-text contradiction modifies Section 2.10 so that the responsibility for equipment supply is shifted to BSP instead of Datec. The original states 'the equipment is to be supplied by Datec and invoiced for payment according to Datec's normal terms of trade'. The modified section omits this and instead explicitly states, 'the equipment is to be supplied by BSP and not invoiced for payment.' This contradicts sections relating to pricing, specifically 8.1, which assumes Datec will be providing the equipment.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 8.1 General Principles The BSP will pay Datec a base fee plus any other fees and charges that may be agreed too during the term of the agreement but excluding, travel, freight and tax related matters. BSP will be responsible for any freight and or related travel costs incurred by Datec in the execution of the services as defined in this agreement. Prior to undertaking or committing to any freight and or related travel costs Datec must first obtain approval from BSP.
perturbation.justification: The modification to Section 2.10 shifts the responsibility for equipment supply from Datec to BSP and stipulates that no invoicing will occur. This directly contradicts Section 8.1, which assumes that Datec will be providing equipment and therefore being paid a fee for it. By stating 'the equipment is to be supplied by BSP and not invoiced for payment', the modified text removes the basis for Datec receiving payment for equipment as contemplated in Section 8.1. The requirement to pay Datec a base fee, as stated in Section 8.1, is predicated on Datec providing equipment.

perturbation.original_text: 14.5 Termination Fee If the BSP terminates under clause 14.3 (Termination by BSP for Convenience) the BSP will pay Datec the Termination Fee calculated in accordance with Attachment C (Pricing).
perturbation.changed_text: 14.5 Termination Fee If the BSP terminates under clause 14.3 (Termination by BSP for Convenience) the BSP will not pay Datec any Termination Fee calculated in accordance with Attachment C (Pricing).
perturbation.explanation: Section 14.5 is directly changed to explicitly state that no termination fee is payable if BSP terminates for convenience. This creates a direct in-text contradiction with Schedule C and clause 14.3, which refer to a termination fee calculation, creating confusion and potential legal disputes.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Schedule C Pricing Schedule Annual Fees Annual Base Fee K3,524,322.00 Monthly Base Fee K293,693.50 SLA Rebate Table % Of SLA Achieved 100% 95% 90% 85% 80% 75% Monthly Rebate % applied 0% 10% 20% 30% 40% 50% • Measured Monthly using the actual Response and Escalation times for logged jobs verse the agreed service times contained in the SLA in Schedule B • Applies to Severity Levels 1 & 2 during normal business hours • Maximum rebate will be 50% of the monthly base fee Termination Fee Table Contract Term Completed 0-Yrl 1-Yr2 2-Yr3 3-Yr4 4-Yr5 5-Yr6 % of Annual Base Fee Payable 50% 40% 30% 20% 10% 0% Note: The Annual Base Fee will be adjusted in accordance with Section 9.1, "CPI Adjustments", of this agreement
perturbation.justification: The changed text in Section 14.5 explicitly states that no termination fee is payable if the BSP terminates for convenience, directly contradicting Schedule C which details the calculation of a termination fee based on the contract term completed. This creates a direct conflict, as one section states a fee is not payable, while another section outlines how that fee is calculated. This inconsistency falls under the definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction', where 'different sections of a contract provide conflicting deadlines, obligations, or penalties,' making it unclear which terms should be enforced and leading to potential disputes.
